# ADR-260010: Adopt Astro Component Templates and Vertical-Slice Feature Convention

Status: Accepted
Date: 2026-06-09
Owners: Repository owner

## Context

The public site has no reusable UI component baseline and contains duplicated CSS across three
policy pages. Adding new features requires copy-pasting markup and styles. A structural decision
is needed on how to organise reusable UI pieces and feature-scoped code going forward.

## Decision

Adopt Astro scoped component templates under `src/components/ui/` (atomic UI) and
`src/components/layout/` (layout wrappers), and introduce `src/features/<name>/` as the
project's vertical-slice convention for all future feature-scoped logic and components.

- Atomic UI components (`Button`, `Card`, `FormField`, `CheckList`) live under `src/components/ui/`.
- Layout wrapper components (`PolicyArticle`) live under `src/components/layout/`.
- Feature-scoped components and logic live under `src/features/<feature-name>/components/` and
  are imported by the corresponding page in `src/pages/`.
- Pages in `src/pages/` may depend on `src/components/`, `src/features/<name>/`, `src/layouts/`,
  and `src/config/` — but never on other pages.

This extends the allowed dependency directions documented in `project-instructions.md` Section 2.

## Alternatives Considered

- **Option A (chosen)**: Astro component files with scoped `<style>` and typed props. Zero new
  tooling. Aligns with Astro's built-in model. Scoping is automatic per component.
- **Option B**: CSS Modules with separate `.module.css` files. Adds tooling overhead and breaks
  Astro's automatic style scoping model.
- **Option C**: Tailwind CSS utility classes. Large dependency and convention change; out of
  scope for this backlog item.

## Consequences

- Positive: Pages gain consistency; new features follow an established pattern; CSS duplication
  is eliminated; no breaking changes to routes or public contracts.
- Negative: Developers must learn the new conventions; there is a small upfront cost to
  refactor existing inline styles where duplication exists.
- Risks: Low. Changes are additive; pages can fall back to inline styles if a component is
  reverted.

## Verification

- All 52 unit tests and 38 e2e tests pass after implementation.
- Visual spot-check confirms `/privacy`, `/terms`, `/google-addon/support` are pixel-identical.
- `/google-addon` check-list renders green ✓ bullets via the CheckList component.
- Sign-in form on `/` and `/sign-in` functions end-to-end.

## Rollback

Delete new component files and restore the inline `<style>` blocks and markup in the three
legal pages and `google-addon.astro`. No routing or contract changes to revert.
